EVIDENCE IN SUPPORT OF OPPOS TO PLTF'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUIDICATION FILED BY DEFENDANT ENGMANN, MICHAEL ENGMANN OPTIONS INC. KENDU PARTNERS BLOGTRONIX LLC
ORDER RE SUMMARY ADJUDICATION GRANTED IN FAVOR OF PLAINTIFF ITBRIX.

LAW AND MOTION 302, THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING WITH THE EXCEPTION CARVED OUT FOR THE ATTORNEY'S FEES AND COSTS NOT ADJUDICATED HERE: PLAINTIFFS IT BRIX LLC AND GEORGE ATHANNASSOV'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION AS TO THE PARTIES' CROSS MOTIONS FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION UNDER CCP SECTION 437C(S), THE COURT FINDS AS FOLLOWS: AS TO ISSUE ONE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED IN FAVOR OF PLAINTIFF ITBRIX. THE UNDISPUTED FACTS SHOW THAT PLAINTIFF TIMELY REQUESTED A LIST OF CURRENT OWNERS UNDER SECTION 18 OF THE TERMINATION AGREEMENT, AND DEFENDANT BLOGTRONIX DID NOT PROVIDE AN ACCURATE LIST OF CURRENT OWNERS IN ACCORDANCE WITH SECTION 18. AS TO ISSUE TWO, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED IN FAVOR OF ITBRIX. BLOGTRONIX HAS FAILED TO PRODUCE SUFFICIENT EVIDENCE THAT THE LIQUIDATED DAMAGES CLAUSE WAS UNREASONABLE AT THE TIME THE TERMINATION AGREEMENT WAS MADE. BLOGTRONIX SHALL PAY $150,000 IN ACCORDANCE WITH THE SECTION 21 OF THE TERMINATION AGREEMENT. AS TO ISSUE THREE, SUMMARY ADJUDICATION IS GRANTED IN FAVOR OF BLOGTRONIX. THE PLAIN LANGUAGE OF SECTION 11C OF THE TERMINATION AGREEMENT STATES THAT ALL CLAIMS ARE RELEASED EXCEPT FOR CLAIMS ARISING FORM THE TERMINATION AGREEMENT, THE LICENSE AGREEMENT, AND THE SOFTWARE DEVELOPMENT AGREEMENT. EACH OF BLOGTRONIX'S PROPOSED CROSS-CLAIMS ARISE FROM THESE AGREEMENTS.AS TO ISSUE FOUR, A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT EXISTS AS TO WHETHER KENDU PARTNERS IS AN "OUTSIDE INVESTOR" UNDER THE TERMS OF SECTION 17 OF THE TERMINATION AGREEMENT. KENDU HAS PRODUCED A DOCUMENT SHOWING THAT OWNERSHIP INTEREST WAS TRANSFERRED FROM BLOGTRONIX TO KENDU PARTNERS IN DECEMBER 2006, WHICH IS OUTSIDE THE INVESTMENT RESTRICTION PERIOD IN THE TERMINATION AGREEMENT. (SEE BLOGTRONIX'S EVIDENCE IN SUPPORT OF OPPOSITION TO ITBRIX'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION, EXH. D.) AS TO ISSUE FIVE, A TRIABLE ISSUE OF FACT EXISTS AS TO WHAT DAMAGES ARE PAYABLE TO ITBRIX AS THE RESULT OF THE TRANSFER OF OWNERSHIP. (SEE MINUTES FOR FULL RULING)
SUBSTITUTION OF ATTORNEY: RUBENSTEIN, SUSAN SUBSTITUTED FOR NEVILLE, DAVE AS ATTORNEY FOR BLOGTRONIX LLC

Plaintiffs It Brix Llc And George Athannassov'S Motion For Summary Adjudication
Off Calendar Sep-27-2013 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
E. Gerard Mannion, Mannion & Lowe, 415.733.1052, appearing for IT Brix;
Dave Neville, 805.640.6468, appearing for Blogtronix.

Matter A R G U E D. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING WITH THE EXCEPTION CARVED OUT FOR THE ATTORNEY'S FEES AND COSTS NOT ADJUDICATED HERE: Plaintiffs IT Brix LLC And George Athannassov's Motion For Summary Adjudication

As to the parties' cross motions for summary adjudication under CCP Section 437c(s), the Court finds as follows:
As to Issue One, summary adjudication is granted in favor of Plaintiff ITBrix. The undisputed facts show that Plaintiff timely requested a list of Current Owners under Section 18 of the Termination Agreement, and Defendant Blogtronix did not provide an accurate list of Current Owners in accordance with Section 18.
As to Issue Two, summary adjudication is granted in favor of ITBrix. Blogtronix has failed to produce sufficient evidence that the liquidated damages clause was unreasonable at the time the Termination Agreement was made. Blogtronix shall pay $150,000 in accordance with the Section 21 of the Termination Agreement.
As to Issue Three, summary adjudication is granted in favor of Blogtronix. The plain language of Section 11c of the Termination Agreement states that all claims are released except for claims arising form the Termination Agreement, the License Agreement, and the Software Development Agreement. Each of Blogtronix's proposed cross-claims arise from these agreements.
As to Issue Four, a triable issue of fact exists as to whether Kendu Partners is an "Outside Investor" under the terms of Section 17 of the Termination Agreement. Kendu has produced a document showing that ownership interest was transferred from Blogtronix to Kendu Partners in December 2006, which is outside the Investment Restriction Period in the Termination Agreement. (See Blogtronix's Evidence in Support of Opposition to ITBrix's Motion for Summary Adjudication, Exh. D.)
As to Issue Five, a triable issue of fact exists as to what damages are payable to ITBrix as the result of the transfer of ownership. Blogtronix has produced evidence to create a triable issue of fact as to whether the transfer of interest occurred outside the Investment Restriction Period in the Termination Agreement. (Id.). Order signed. Judge: Leslie C. Nichols, Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185. =(302/LCN)
